title: 526 Israeli Settlers Storm Al-Aqsa Mosque Compound on Israel's 78th Independence Day
detail: On April 22, 2026 — Israel's 78th Independence Day (Yom HaAtzmaut) — 526 Israeli settlers stormed the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound (Haram al-Sharif) in East Jerusalem under Israeli police escort. Settlers raised Israeli flags inside the compound courtyard and performed Talmudic prayers and prostration rituals in the eastern section, including near the Dome of the Rock. The Jerusalem Islamic Waqf issued an urgent protest statement condemning the incursions as violations of the status quo agreement governing the holy site. Pakistan's Foreign Ministry formally condemned the incursion as 'a blatant violation of international law.' The Jerusalem Governorate described the incursions as 'a dangerous bid to impose Israeli control over Al-Aqsa.' The storming is part of an escalating pattern: National Security Minister Ben-Gvir personally led an incursion on April 12 — his third in 2026 — and settler visits under police escort have become near-daily during Jewish holidays. The April 22 figure of 526 settlers represents one of the highest single-day incursion counts of 2026.

media.caption: Israeli settlers storm Al-Aqsa Mosque compound on Israel's 78th Independence Day, April 22, 2026

title: Palestinian Shot Dead by Israeli Settlers in Deir Dibwan; 30 Detained
detail: Israeli settlers shot and killed one Palestinian in Deir Dibwan, a village east of Ramallah in the occupied West Bank, on April 22, 2026, according to WAFA (the official Palestinian news agency). Multiple other Palestinians were injured in the attack, and Israeli occupation forces detained approximately 30 people from the area during the same incident. Israeli forces also separately detained individuals from Nablus and Jenin in the day's broader operations. The Deir Dibwan killing comes one day after settlers killed a 14-year-old student and a 32-year-old man at al-Mughayyir Boys School northeast of Ramallah (April 21), and forms part of an intensifying wave of settler violence across the West Bank during Israel's Independence Day period. April 21–22 now represents one of the most lethal consecutive settler-violence episodes in 2026, with at least three Palestinians killed in two days.

media.caption: Palestinian shot dead by settlers in Deir Dibwan, east of Ramallah, April 22, 2026

title: Hamas Formally Rejects US 'Board of Peace' Disarmament Proposal in Cairo Talks
detail: Hamas formally rejected the US-led Board of Peace's disarmament proposal in ongoing Cairo negotiations on April 22, 2026. Hamas negotiators stated they would not discuss disarmament until Israel fulfills its Phase 1 commitments — including increased humanitarian aid flow and freedom of movement within Gaza — and until the outcome of separate Iran-related ceasefire negotiations is clarified. Hamas also demanded substantial modifications to the Gaza governance framework proposed by the Board of Peace before any disarmament talks could proceed. The Gaza Government Media Office has documented more than 2,073 ceasefire violations (airstrikes, gunfire, and incursions) since the October 2025 ceasefire was announced. The rejection marks a significant impasse in the US-brokered reconstruction and governance negotiations that have been underway since the Board of Peace's inaugural meeting in Washington in February 2026. The Trump administration had sought Hamas disarmament as a precondition for broader reconstruction funding.

media.caption: Hamas rejects US Board of Peace disarmament proposal in Cairo ceasefire negotiations, April 2026

title: Five Major NGOs Release 'Failing' Scorecard on Gaza Ceasefire at Six-Month Mark
detail: A joint scorecard released on April 22, 2026 by five major humanitarian organizations — Danish Refugee Council, Norwegian Refugee Council, Oxfam, Refugees International, and Save the Children — assessed the Gaza ceasefire as 'failing' six months after its October 10, 2025 launch. Key findings: at least 2 children per day are being killed or injured since the ceasefire; aid truck entries to Gaza fell 80% in the first two weeks of March 2026, raising renewed famine risk; basic goods prices have surged dramatically (food price inflation at 305% in March); UNRWA remains blocked from bringing international staff into Gaza since March 2025; and approximately 11,000 Palestinian UNRWA staff continue operations without adequate supply. OHCHR also released a statement noting 'Palestinians across Gaza [are] unsafe six months on from ceasefire announcement.' The signatories called on Israel to immediately allow unrestricted humanitarian access and demanded international accountability for ceasefire violations. Cumulative Gaza deaths since October 7, 2023 stand at approximately 72,315–72,553, with 738–777 Palestinians killed since the October 2025 ceasefire.

media.caption: Humanitarian organizations release six-month ceasefire scorecard showing conditions in Gaza are 'failing'
